title: calamagrostis gigas var. aspera
intro: Calamagrostis gigas var. aspera, also known as the rough meadow-grass, is a type of ornamental grass native to mountainous regions. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, and is said to have astringent and diuretic effects. The health benefits of Calamagrostis gigas var. aspera include its ability to treat digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water, and it can also be used as a tincture or in tea form.
